Talk to reporters at the White House, the US President said that he believes "it's very possible that we'll make a deal," saying that the #US and Iran "had very good talks over the last 24 hours."

#Trump repeated claims of having depleted Iran's military capabilities, alleging, "They don’t have any planes… no anti-aircraft… no radar left."

He further alleged that "their missiles are mostly decimated," claiming, "I think we won."

Reiterating his demand, Trump said, "#Iran cannot have a nuclear weapon… and they won’t."

#IranWar #WarOnIran

"الولايات المتحدة لا يمكن أن تتخلى عن دول مجلس التعاون الخليجي التي تُعدّ جزءاً أساسياً من اقتصادها، وفي حال كفّت يدها عن هذه المنطقة، فإنّ الاقتصاد الأميركي قد يتضعضع بشكل كبير."

In an exclusive interview for Al Mayadeen, Iran’s Expediency Discernment Council member, Major General Mohsen Rezaei, argued that the #US is repeating a failed post–Cold War playbook in the region. 

From former President George W. Bush to President Donald Trump, Rezaei said US attempts to dominate the region’s power balance have led to a "dead end".

Rezaei further insisted the "Persian Gulf must remain independent."

#IranWar #WarOnIran #Iran

@IRIMFA_SPOX :
Le concept de « négociations » exige, au minimum, une tentative sincère d’engager des discussions en vue de résoudre le différend (CIJ, Arrêt du 1er avril 2011, par. 157). Il requiert donc une « bonne foi », ce qui signifie que les « négociations » ne sont pas une « dispute » ;

The only viable solution in the Strait of Hormuz is clear: a permanent end to the war, the lifting of the maritime blockade, and the restoration of normal passage.

Instead, the U.S. is pushing a flawed, politically motivated UN Security Council draft resolution under the pretext of “freedom of navigation” to advance its political agenda and legitimise unlawful actions—not to resolve the crisis.

Iran calls on Member States to act on the basis of logic, fairness and principle, not pressure, reject the draft, and refrain from supporting or co-sponsoring it.
اهانت به تمثال مبارک حضرت مریم (سلام الله‌علیها) توسط یک نظامی اسرائیلی در جنوب لبنان https://t.co/PCuDGHdxry

World Bank: Strait of Hormuz traffic will not normalize until late 2026

https://t.co/GRloPpjkSE

US President Donald Trump announced a temporary pause of “Project Freedom,” a US naval initiative aimed at ensuring navigation through the Strait of Hormuz, citing progress toward a potential agreement with Iran.

“Based on the request of Pakistan and other Countries, the tremendous Military Success that we have had during the Campaign against the Country of Iran and, additionally, the fact that Great Progress has been made toward a Complete and Final Agreement with Representatives of Iran, we have mutually agreed that, while the Blockade will remain in full force and effect, Project Freedom (The Movement of Ships through the Strait of Hormuz) will be paused for a short period of time to see whether or not the Agreement can be finalized and signed,” Trump wrote on Truth Social.

The announcement follows a brief and intense phase of US military activity tied to the initiative, which lasted roughly 48 hours before being halted. The operation had been presented by Washington as a step to secure maritime routes amid escalating tensions with Iran, despite it being yet another inflammatory move by the Trump administration.

According to sources cited by Al Mayadeen, no US or other vessels have successfully passed through the Strait of Hormuz in recent days, despite US claims of resumed maritime transit.

Iran blasts the UAE for collusion with US forces, warning Abu Dhabi it will pay the price for undermining regional security and aligning with hostile powers.

https://t.co/0B4kddrxj8
